scope.summary: Implement estimate_fabric_params MCP tool with DiffXPBD differentiable XPBD gradient-descent backend: accepts a target drape image artifact, runs forward-backward passes to optimise FabricProperties to match the observed drape, returns a suggested edit_garment patch
acceptance_criteria: ClothSolver trait gains an optional diff_estimate_fabric method (default impl returns ClothSolverError::NoAdapter when the diff-xpbd feature flag is disabled)

When diff-xpbd feature is enabled, the solver runs gradient descent on normalized FabricProperties fields; the non-linear map to raw XPBD compliance is applied at mesh-build time inside the solver, not exposed to the optimiser

estimate_fabric_params MCP tool accepts draft_id and target_image_artifact_id; the tool fetches the image from ArtifactStore, renders a simulated drape, computes pixel-space or feature-space loss against the target image, and backpropagates through the solver
The tool returns a SuggestedFabricParams receipt containing a JSON Merge Patch fragment targeting /fabric, directly usable in an edit_garment call
The gradient descent loop is bounded by max_iterations (default 50) and a convergence threshold; it returns the best-found params even if not fully converged
The estimate_fabric_params run is dispatched through TailorSandboxAdapter (SandboxRunV1 lifecycle) and emits TailorSimRunRequested -> TailorSimRunCompleted events
The tool is gated behind the diff-xpbd Cargo feature and returns a typed error (RunFailed with message 'diff-xpbd feature not enabled') when the feature is absent
proof_targets: cargo test -p tailor-solver --features diff-xpbd diff_xpbd::fabric_gradient (gradient descent reduces drape loss on a synthetic silk vs denim discrimination test)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::estimate_fabric_params_dispatch (tool dispatches through sandbox and emits correct event sequence)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::estimate_fabric_params_feature_off (returns typed error when diff-xpbd disabled)
scope.risk_if_missed: The inverse fabric parameter path is absent. When preset-based forward simulation produces visually wrong drape and no NL description is adequate, there is no data-driven way to find the correct FabricProperties. The Dress-1-to-3 differentiable-XPBD moat — which makes Handshake the only tool where a model can match observed real-world cloth behavior from an image — is lost. This is the highest-value differentiator for production garment matching.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
